title: Evangelion Dub vs Sub Comparison Videos
display_title: Evangelion Dub vs Sub Comparison Videos
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Dub vs. Sub Comparison Video(s)             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Mar 24, 2016 8:38 am Hey guys, last week there was a discussion over on Reddit where I think someone was asking whether they should watch Eva in sub or in dub, and it reminded me of the classroom scene where they added some additional slapstick dialogue which changed the context of the scene from tense/serious, to more slapstick/comedy. I made a video putting both versions back to back, and I'm thinking of doing some more comparisons like this. Anyone interested? https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7vMk_jJzScw Also, subscribe to me on YouTube, I just might be making some Eva videos in the near future. Plz k thx bai.

[]: Re: Evangelion Dub vs. Sub Comparison Video(s)             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 24, 2016 8:56 am Do the scene in episode 5 where they have Shinji say "Bullshit!" for absolutely no reason at all. ( Added it to the Platinum version where it was originally and rightfully absent, even.) Epitome of smart and reasoned adaptation, there...

[]: Re: Evangelion Dub vs. Sub Comparison Video(s)             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Mar 24, 2016 10:38 am I will definitely keep that flagged. With these, I'm trying to keep away from things like Shinji and Misato's use of the F word in End of Eva, because I can concede that in those situations, that would be a natural way of expressing things, but anything that is deliberately changed, altered, added, etc is what I'd like to focus on. Like all those silly smuckers jam sound effects they added, or dialogue they added, like "Hit em again!" with the flame thrower.

[]: Re: Evangelion Dub vs. Sub Comparison Video(s)             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Mar 24, 2016 11:18 am Yeah, no problems there. The "Bullshit!" thing is exactly the sort of thing you want -- it corresponds to precisely nothing in the original Japanese. As it's been forever since I've seen the dub, that's the only super-obvious example of "added dialogue" that comes to mind. As for "changed", I remember a relatively recent discussion where Asuka's date with the "med student" was brought up. Turned out there was nothing in the Japanese even implying "med student".

[]: Re: Evangelion Dub vs. Sub Comparison Video(s)             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Mar 24, 2016 6:12 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: it reminded me of the classroom scene where they added some additional slapstick dialogue which changed the context of the scene from tense/serious, to more slapstick/comedy.

[]: Re: Evangelion Dub vs. Sub Comparison Video(s)             Quote Post by BC Baron » Thu Mar 24, 2016 7:00 pm ^ I think Tiffany Grant may have thrown in a couple of German expletives or something toward the end of the clip, but that's far from the only scene where she's heard doing that.

[]: Re: Evangelion Dub vs. Sub Comparison Video(s)             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Mar 25, 2016 9:04 am pwhodges wrote: I suppose Asuka's a bit more noisy in the English dub, but I'm not noticing slapstick or extra dialogue. What do you think I might be missing?
